figures.caption: Table 4: Absence rates by organ type and selected topic categories. The absence rate is the fraction of question– handbook pairs in which the handbook did not address the question (Eq. 1).
Figure 2: Distribution of benchmark questions by organ type and topic category.
Figure 3: Overview of the experimental pipeline.

Figure 4: Selected comparison matrices illustrating heterogeneity patterns. Each cell represents a pairwise comparison between two center handbooks for a single question. Colors: grey = ABSENT, green = CONSISTENT, yellow = COMPLEMENTARY, blue = DIVERGENT, black = CONTRADICTORY. Handbook labels on axes are anonymized (organ-center index-phase). Matrices are symmetric; diagonal entries are CONSISTENT by convention.
Table 6: Topic-level heterogeneity metrics (topics with ≥20 questions). Columns as in Table 5.
Table 2: Five-label taxonomy for pairwise comparison of center-specific answers.
